summary: DHH announces that the Danish startup formerly known as MetroStar* has rebranded as Organic and launched its online presence. The company aims to be a new kind of telecommunications provider focused on being a catalyst for mobile internet access. DHH contrasts Organic's idealistic mission with traditional telcos, suggesting the startup's founding team of entrepreneurs and Danish bloggers gives it credibility. He predicts Organic will drive WiFi adoption in Copenhagen.
key_points: A Danish telecom startup rebranded from MetroStar* to Organic with a new online identity
Organic positions itself as a bandwidth catalyst rather than a traditional telco, focused on connecting people to mobile internet
The founding team includes entrepreneurs, key Danish bloggers, and business veterans, lending credibility
DHH is skeptical of traditional telcos keeping their ideals beyond their next brand campaign
money_quotes: Since they're not written by your usual telco there's actually a chance they'll keep them in mind longer than their next brand campaign.
Organic is poised to become the reason for Copenhageners to pick up a wifi card in the next six to twelve months.
key_insight: A startup founded by entrepreneurs and community figures rather than corporate telecom insiders has a better chance of staying true to its mission of democratizing mobile internet access.
